title: Power-Law Scaling
concept: Model performance scales as a power-law with respect to parameter count, dataset size, and compute budget, with no sign of flattening out.

exercises.prompt: What are the three main resources that scale together in AI scaling laws?

options.text: Compute, dataset size, and parameter count
Coding language, screen size, and internet speed
User location, cost of chips, and AI safety rules

exercises.explain: Power-law scaling links three main ingredients: computing power (compute), the size of the training dataset, and the number of parameters (model size).

cards.title: Predicting AI Smarts
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how tech companies knew that building giant models like GPT-4 would actually work before they spent millions of dollars on electricity? They relied on a mathematical superpower called Power-Law Scaling!

cards.title: What is Power-Law Scaling?
cards.body: Power-Law Scaling is a mathematical relationship showing that as you feed an AI model more compute power, more data, and more parameters, its performance improves at a predictable, steady rate without hitting a hard limit.

cards.title: The AI Athlete
cards.body: Think of training an AI like training an athlete. If you double their training time, they don't get twice as fast. Instead, their improvement follows a predictable, steady curve. Power-law scaling is the math rulebook that tells us exactly how much more "food" (data and compute) we need to feed our AI "athlete" to get them to the next skill level.

cards.title: Predicting the Future
cards.body: In 2020, researchers at OpenAI published a paper showing that if you increase a language model's size from 10 million parameters to 100 billion parameters, its loss (error rate) drops following a straight line on a logarithmic chart. This predictability allows engineers to estimate how smart a model will be before they even spend millions of dollars to train it.

cards.title: Myth: AI Will Hit a Wall Soon
cards.body: Many people believe that AI models will quickly hit a wall where adding more data or computer chips stops making them smarter. However, scaling laws show that performance keeps improving steadily as we scale up, with no signs of flattening out yet.

cards.title: The Power-Law Line
cards.body: When we plot scaling on normal graphs, the curve looks like it's slowing down. But when we look at it on a logarithmic scale, we see a beautiful, straight line of continuous improvement.

left.text: Shows a steady, straight line of predictable improvement as resources scale up.

right.text: Looks like progress is slowing down because the numbers get so large.

exercises.prompt: What does the concept of Power-Law Scaling tell us about AI models?

options.text: Model performance improves predictably as compute, data, and parameters increase.
AI models randomly get smarter without needing more data.
Models stop getting better once they reach a certain size.
Smaller models are always more accurate than larger models.

exercises.explain: Power-law scaling describes how AI performance improves predictably as we scale up compute, data, and model parameters.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Power-law scaling shows that AI performance has already hit a hard limit and stopped improving.
exercises.explain: There is currently no evidence that scaling laws are flattening out; models continue to get better as we scale them up.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how AI performance scales.
exercises.sentence: Because of scaling laws, as we add more compute and data, the improvement in AI performance is highly ____________.
exercises.explain: Power-law scaling makes AI improvement highly predictable, allowing researchers to calculate future performance before spending money on training.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements about scaling laws is accurate and generated by an AI expert?

options.text: AI: "Scaling laws prove that AI models will eventually become sentient once they pass one trillion parameters."
AI: "According to scaling laws, AI performance scales smoothly as a power-law of compute budget, dataset size, and parameter count."
AI: "Scaling laws state that you only need to increase the dataset size to make a model smarter, while keeping parameters the same."

exercises.explain: The prompt generated by the AI is the one that correctly identifies the three core pillars of scaling laws: compute, data, and parameter count.

exercises.prompt: To get the full benefits of power-law scaling when building larger AI models, which three resources must you scale up together?

options.text: Only the number of parameters matters for performance
Only the dataset size matters for performance
Parameters, dataset size, and compute budget must all scale together
AI models stop improving once they reach 1 billion parameters

exercises.explain: Power-law scaling rules apply to three main ingredients: the number of parameters (model size), the amount of training data, and the total compute budget. Increasing just one without the others leads to bottlenecks.
